summary: Tight China>Rider opener, soaring Eyes, vibrant Iko jam
blurb: The first set launches with a rare China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and the tight Bird Song → The Music Never Stopped → Might As Well sequence. The second set delivers a high‑energy drums‑Iko segment, an improvisational stretch on Eyes Of The World, and a sweet encore featuring Hey Bo Diddley. Overall a solid 1986 Kaiser performance with plenty of jam highlights.
review: Feb 11, 1986 at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center captured the Dead in top form. A rare China Cat Sunflower into I Know You Rider opened the night, immediately setting a joyous tone that carried through a first‑set groove‑fest highlighted by Bird Song → The Music Never Stopped → Might As Well. Brent’s funky keyboards and Jerry’s gritty vocals kept the momentum rolling into an expansive second set where the Rhythm Devils unleashed a powerful Drums → Iko Iko jam, followed by a soaring Eyes Of The World that many reviewers called “free‑form improvisational flow.” The band closed with a sweet encore—Hey Bo Diddley and Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad—that left the crowd buzzing. While some listeners noted uneven pacing in the latter half, the crisp soundboard capture (and even better matrix mixes) preserve every nuance, making this show a must‑listen for fans of the mid‑80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are clearly positive (soundboard and matrix) praising the China>Rider opener, Bird Song > Music > Might As Well trio, and energetic drum jams; three mixed reviews note minor unevenness but no consistent criticism. The overall sentiment is strongly favorable, warranting a moderate‑high adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent, crisp soundboard mix that captures the standout jams (China>Rider, Bird Song > Music > Might As Well, Iko jam) with clear instrument separation.

key_highlights: Rare China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider opener not heard in years
Tight Bird Song → The Music Never Stopped → Might As Well sequence
Powerful Drums → Iko Iko segment driving the second set energy
Soaring, free‑form Eyes Of The World improvisation

band_performance.Jerry: Gravelly, character‑filled vocals and solid, fun guitar tone; especially lively on the China>Rider opener and Eyes jam.
band_performance.Phil: Steady, melodic bass foundation with a standout line during Bird Song → Music → Might As Well.
band_performance.Bob: Provides reliable rhythm support and backup vocals, contributing to the overall tightness without any notable issues.
band_performance.Brent: Funky keyboard solos and noodling on China/Rider, plus vocal harmonies that lift the jam sections throughout the show.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivers powerful dual‑drum work, ‘screaming’ during the Aiko/Drums‑Iko jams and keeping the rhythmic drive high.
band_performance.Billy: Strong, energetic drumming alongside Mickey; drives the second‑set drum jams with intensity.
